description_long: This filing cabinet features a tall, rectangular form with a warm, woodgrain finish, likely made from laminated wood or veneer. The front face is divided into several horizontal sections, each suggesting the presence of individual drawers, though no external handles or label holders are visible. Subtle horizontal grooves or inlays mark the separation between drawers, giving the cabinet a minimalist and modern appearance. The sides are narrow and flat, consistent with a standard vertical filing cabinet design. The surface appears smooth and slightly glossy, reflecting light gently. This cabinet would typically be found in an office or archive setting, providing organized storage for documents.
description: A tall, wood-finished filing cabinet with multiple horizontal drawer divisions and a smooth, modern surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height, woodgrain finish, and horizontal divisions indicating drawers.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the cabinet's narrow profile and flat, featureless side panel.
description_view_2: Rear or alternate front view, similar to the main front, with visible woodgrain and drawer divisions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim, flat side of the cabinet.
